current_fact.fact_text: The Confederate States of America, whose lost cruiser SS Georgiana lay hidden until 1965, existed for just four years yet left an imprint on symbols, songs, and identity that still sparks debate. Its short, bloody bid for nationhood continues to echo in arguments about memory, monuments, and what flags really mean.

episode.title: Morning Drops #44 - Residente
episode.description:
In this edition of Morning Drops, Yasmin navigates the complex geography of René Pérez Joglar, known to the world as Residente. His story is not just a musical evolution; it is a timeline marked by resistance, lyrical obsession, and a relentless search for identity.

From the gated streets of Trujillo Alto to the global stage, we explore how a restless mind transformed into the voice of a generation. The selection moves through the textures of his life: hearing the raw energy of his early rebellion, the sharp precision of his political critique, and finally, the fragile silence of his most personal confessions.

It is a study in duality. We witness the rapper who dismantles giants and the son who misses his mother. It is a soundscape that proves that sometimes, the loudest revolution happens inside.

Mood: Raw, Revolutionary, Introspective, Honest
Music genre: Latin Alternative, Hip Hop, Urban, World

current_fact.match_reason: A breakaway state whose symbolism still fuels culture wars connects to an artist obsessed with identity, resistance, and dismantling political myths through lyrics.

queue.fact_text: C‑SPAN’s first broadcast of the U.S. House in 1979 marked a quiet revolution: for the first time, citizens could watch representatives argue in real time instead of reading filtered reports. The cameras, always on but never zooming in too close, created a new kind of stage where democracy looked more like a long, unscripted jam session than a polished speech.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
episode.description: The first Family & Friends radio episode from Yasmin Sarbaoui and Gaia Parolini. Where it all began — a life soundtrack that launched Sedna FM into the universe. Raw, heartfelt, and full of the energy that defines everything that followed.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como pelicula, tragedia, comedia y ficcion

queue.match_reason: The first unfiltered broadcast of a political chamber aligns with Sedna FM’s inaugural episode—both openings of a channel where voices, debates, and rhythms flow directly to listeners.

published.fact_text: In 1824, American sealer and explorer Benjamin Morrell sailed away from Antarctica with tales so dramatic that later historians accused him of exaggeration or outright invention. His controversial accounts include descriptions of mysterious lands and colossal seal hunts that modern scholars struggle to verify, blurring the line between exploration log and adventure fiction.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

published.match_reason: An Antarctic voyage full of disputed sightings pairs with an episode about immersion and fluid, watery soundscapes, echoing the feeling of slipping beyond known maps.

published.fact_text: On March 19, 1965, a teenage diver named E. Lee Spence stumbled upon the wreck of the Confederate cruiser SS Georgiana—exactly 102 years to the day after it sank. The ship, once touted as the most powerful Confederate commerce raider, lay hidden beneath the waves with a cargo whose modern value has been estimated in the tens of millions of dollars.

episode.title: Evening Flows #01 - Yasmin - Crescendo in the Medina
episode.description: The first Evening Flow episode comes from Yasmin. It starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, textured, and grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. A journey through ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

published.match_reason: A mysterious shipwreck discovered under the sea fits with a nocturnal, slowly unfolding mix that starts in the depths and rises like a treasure brought to the surface.

published.fact_text: In 1979, the daily debates, deals, and deadlocks of the United States House of Representatives suddenly became visible to anyone with cable when C‑SPAN began broadcasting its sessions. For the first time, viewers could watch lawmakers live and unedited, turning parliamentary procedure into an unscripted, long-running public show.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

published.match_reason: Televising Congress transformed how citizens connect to politics, much like the episode reflects on our evolving relationship with electronic media and constant connectivity.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1687, French explorer René-Robert Cavelier, Sieur de La Salle—who had dreamed of building a New France empire at the mouth of the Mississippi—was murdered by his own men in the wilds of Texas. His death turned a grand imperial project into a cautionary tale about leadership, navigation errors, and the brutal stakes of exploration.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai

published.match_reason: A doomed overland expedition and a show about journeys through landscapes and stories both meditate on how travel shapes destiny and memory.

published.fact_text: On March 19, 2011, as Libyan government forces threatened the rebel stronghold of Benghazi, French jets roared in under Opération Harmattan, striking armored columns on the road. It was the opening act of a multinational intervention that turned a domestic uprising into a war watched live around the world.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

published.match_reason: Foreign airstrikes in a civil uprising mirror the episode’s focus on political resistance and the collision between power, protest, and amplified noise.

published.fact_text: In 1982, long before the Falklands War became a headline, Argentine commandos quietly landed on South Georgia Island, a remote speck in the South Atlantic. Their occupation of an abandoned whaling station there—seemingly minor—set off a chain reaction that would send fleets and soldiers into icy seas thousands of kilometers from home.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

published.match_reason: A frigid island skirmish in the South Atlantic connects naturally with an episode steeped in polar seas, cold light, and the vastness of remote waters.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1279, the Mongol-led Yuan fleet crushed the last Song dynasty forces at the Battle of Yamen, a massive naval clash fought among islands and narrow channels. The defeated child emperor is said to have been carried into the sea by a loyal official, ending over three centuries of Song rule in a single, tragic plunge.

episode.title: Morning Drops #16 - The Sound of Tuareg
episode.description: A journey into the sound of the Tuareg: from traditional chants and desert rituals to the electrified rhythms of exile and resistance. Music shaped by sand, memory, and survival.

published.match_reason: An empire falling in a coastal battle and music born from nomadic resistance both tell stories of people reshaped by conquest, survival, and long memory.

published.fact_text: On March 19, 1945, a single Japanese dive bomber hit the USS Franklin so hard that 724 sailors were killed and the carrier nearly destroyed. Yet the crew managed to save their flaming, listing ship and coax her all the way across the Pacific back to the United States under her own power.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

published.match_reason: The sudden, devastating impact of a dive-bombing run echoes the episode’s theme of jolting, high-voltage energy and survival through raw power.

published.fact_text: In 1944, Nazi Germany abruptly occupied its own ally, Hungary, in a meticulously planned operation triggered by fears that Budapest might seek a separate peace. On March 19, German troops rolled in without declaring war, seizing control of the country’s fate in a matter of hours.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

published.match_reason: A sudden, coercive ‘change of destination’ for a nation pairs with an episode framed as a journey through shifting territories and altered perspectives.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1205, Latin Crusader forces under Henry of Flanders defeated the Nicaean army at the Battle of Adramyttion, in what is now Turkey. The clash was part of a strange moment when Western knights ruled Constantinople, and rival Byzantine states battled not just for land, but for the right to claim a shattered empire’s legacy.

episode.title: Morning Drops #36 - Women of Rock
episode.description: A tribute to the women who shaped the sound of rock, the ones who picked up a guitar and refused to ask for permission. The conversation unfolds in Italian, led by Gaia and her father. A dialogue between generations and a reminder that the pulse of rock has always belonged to anyone brave enough to play it loud. Distortion, rhythm, freedom.
songs: Joan Jett & The Blackhearts - I Love Rock 'n Roll

published.match_reason: A battle over imperial identity and who gets to rule parallels an episode about claiming space and power in a traditionally gatekept arena—this time with guitars instead of swords.

published.fact_text: In 2008, a gamma-ray burst labeled GRB 080319B lit up the constellation Boötes so intensely that, for about 40 seconds, it became the most distant object ever visible to the naked eye. The explosion happened 7.5 billion light-years away, meaning Earth was seeing a cosmic catastrophe that occurred long before our planet even formed.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A record-breaking cosmic flash that briefly appears in the empty sky resonates with an episode about reimagining emptiness as creative, outer-space-like possibility.

published.fact_text: On March 19, 1969, the 385‑meter Emley Moor transmitting mast in England suddenly collapsed under the weight of ice that had built up on its structure. The fall twisted the huge steel cables and shattered the tower, briefly silencing a major TV signal and becoming a textbook case of how weather can outmatch engineering.

episode.title: Evening Flows #00 - Introduction to evening flows
episode.description:
Sedna FM introduces a new format: Evening Flow, where you can enjoy curated music sets from selectors, DJs, and artists.

Each mix is an invitation to drift beyond the expected. A space where hidden gems from personal archives come to light, where sound speaks its own language. There are no rules here, no patterns to follow. Just the steady unfolding of a mood, a texture, a journey shaped by feeling.

Let the music lead the way - enjoy the trip!

published.match_reason: A colossal TV mast crashing down and temporarily muting broadcasts pairs with the birth of a new, more fluid broadcast format, as if signal and silence rewrote themselves.

published.fact_text: In 1918, the United States Congress passed the Standard Time Act, officially carving the country into time zones and endorsing daylight saving time. Overnight, millions of people had to reset their clocks and rethink their relationship with sunrise, sunset, and the shared rhythm of modern life.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

published.match_reason: An act that mechanizes time and speeds up industrial life contrasts gently with an episode inviting us to slow the planet back down and reclaim our own tempo.

published.fact_text: On March 19, 2023, in the midst of a spiraling banking crisis, the Swiss government brokered an emergency deal for UBS to acquire Credit Suisse, fusing two financial giants once seen as rivals. The overnight merger aimed to prevent global market panic, turning Zurich into an impromptu financial triage center.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description:
Hosted by Yasmin, this Morning Drop explores learning as a lifelong process, focusing on persistence, repetition, and the emotional landscape that comes with challenging oneself over time.

Through personal stories and scientific insights, the episode looks at how learning reshapes the brain, generates joy through effort, and why progress often arrives after confusion and frustration. It reflects on the so-called delayed pleasure effect, the role of dopamine in motivation, and how curiosity and learning support long-term wellbeing.

The episode also questions our cultural relationship with failure and invites a softer, more realistic approach to learning, one that accepts mistakes as part of growth and sees persistence as a quiet form of strength.

Mood: reflective, grounded, quietly hopeful
Music genre: alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, neo-soul, drill

A space to listen slowly and stay with the process.

published.match_reason: A crisis-driven merger and the need to rethink risk and failure fit with an episode about learning from mistakes and restructuring how we understand long-term resilience.

published.fact_text: In 2004, the president of Taiwan, Chen Shui-bian, was shot in an apparent assassination attempt while riding in an open jeep on the eve of a tightly contested election. The bullet grazed his abdomen, and he returned to the campaign amid swirling rumors and political tension that gripped the island.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

published.match_reason: A sudden, life-threatening injury to a leader just before a crucial vote parallels an episode anchored in bodily vulnerability, slow recovery, and the emotional aftermath of shock.

published.fact_text: On March 19, 1990, ethnic clashes erupted in Târgu Mureș, Romania, between Hungarian and Romanian communities, just months after the fall of communism. The violence, centered in a single city square, exposed long-suppressed tensions and showed how old grievances can resurface when political systems change overnight.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

published.match_reason: Localized ethnic violence after a regime change contrasts with an episode about movement and perspective, hinting at how travel and reflection can soften hardened borders in the mind.

published.fact_text: In 1989, the Egyptian flag was finally raised over the tiny coastal town of Taba, ending more than two decades of Israeli occupation since the Six-Day War. The handover, settled through maps, arbitration, and diplomacy rather than tanks, became a symbol of the slow work of peace after rapid war.

episode.title: Morning Drops #11 - Mediterranean Boat
episode.description: Gaia invites you to embark on a musical voyage across the Mediterranean. Exploring music from different countries shared by people who call them home. A warm, sun-soaked journey through coastal sounds.
songs: Fabrizio De André - Crêuza de mä

published.match_reason: A coastal border finally resolved on the Red Sea connects with a musical voyage across Mediterranean shores, both celebrating contested coastlines turned into places of passage.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1958, a fire swept through the Monarch Underwear Company in Manhattan, trapping workers on the upper floors and killing 24 people. The tragedy echoed earlier garment-factory disasters, highlighting that even in the age of skyscrapers, basic workplace safety could still be fatally neglected.

episode.title: Morning Drops #01 - Timeless Legacy
episode.description: Gaia guides us through timeless music to start the morning with the right foot! The first episode of the Morning Drops series — classic sounds that never fade.
songs: Roberta Flack - Killing me softly with his song

published.match_reason: A haunting industrial fire becomes a quiet reminder of human cost, paired with an episode of soulful 'timeless legacy' songs that carry memory and tenderness across decades.

published.fact_text: In 1946, France transformed four far-flung territories—French Guiana, Guadeloupe, Martinique, and Réunion—into full overseas departments, theoretically placing them on equal footing with regions in mainland Europe. In one legal stroke, equatorial forests, Caribbean beaches, and Indian Ocean volcanoes were folded more tightly into the French Republic.

episode.title: Morning Drops #18 - Sardegna
episode.description: Yasmin traces her connection to Sardegna — the island where her Italian journey began. Through music and memory, she shares the story of how this place shaped her family's path, and why it still holds such a deep place in her heart.
songs: Mahmood & Tenores di Bitti - Com’è profondo il mare

published.match_reason: Island territories becoming integral parts of a European nation dovetail with an episode devoted to the emotional geography of an island and what it means to belong to it.

published.fact_text: Also on March 19, 1945, Adolf Hitler signed the so‑called Nero Decree, ordering that Germany’s own infrastructure—bridges, factories, railways—be destroyed to deny them to the advancing Allies. The apocalyptic order effectively called for the self-destruction of the country he claimed to save, though many officials quietly refused to fully carry it out.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

published.match_reason: A decree of deliberate ruin near war’s end is countered by an episode about storms passing, resilience, and the refusal to let destruction have the last word.

published.fact_text: The Mongol conquest of the Song dynasty, sealed at Yamen in 1279, created not just a new Chinese empire but a Eurasian network where ideas, goods, and diseases traveled at unprecedented speed. For merchants and scholars, this "Pax Mongolica" was both terrifying and exhilarating—a fragile global web born from the fall of a dynasty.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

published.match_reason: The end of one empire giving rise to a new, globally connected order mirrors how Afrobeat emerged by hybridizing traditions into a networked, world-shaping sound.

published.fact_text: The 2011 Libyan Civil War’s Second Battle of Benghazi turned a coastal city into a focal point where local uprisings, armored columns, and foreign airpower collided. For a few days, the fate of an entire regime depended on what happened at a handful of checkpoints, roundabouts, and runways on the edge of the Mediterranean.

episode.title: Morning Drops #04 - Bars With Balls
episode.description: Yasmin dives into Women's Day through the voices of four incredible female rappers. Morning fire from the magic planet of Sedna FM — move your hands up and down with us. Hip hop culture through a feminine lens.
songs: Little Simz - Point and Kill (feat. Obongjayar)

published.match_reason: Street-level battles over who gets to speak for a country resonate with an episode about assertive, resistant voices claiming space and power through rap.

published.fact_text: The Confederate States of America, whose lost cruiser SS Georgiana lay hidden until 1965, existed for just four years yet left an imprint on symbols, songs, and identity that still sparks debate. Its short, bloody bid for nationhood continues to echo in arguments about memory, monuments, and what flags really mean.
